verdict: iter2 resolves both iter1 findings (Finding 1: M5 ..HEAD corrected; Finding 2: M4/AC3 extended to files_created via ls-files), but the fix to Finding 2 is INCOMPLETE: iter2 extended the QA CHECK side (M4/AC3) to validate files_created via git ls-files, but left the DERIVATION side (M2 + agents/dev.md) unchanged. M2 still mandates that files_created be derived from git diff --name-only, which cannot enumerate untracked new files. M5 similarly applies the git diff filter to both files_modified and files_created in the changelog-analyst provenance filter — new untracked files would be falsely classified as provenance_anomaly by changelog-analyst. This internal inconsistency (QA checks files_created via ls-files; spec tells dev to derive files_created via git diff) was introduced by iter2's partial scope of fix and must be resolved before the spec can proceed to dev.

finding_1_m5_head_form.evidence: M5 (ticket line 84) no longer contains any ..HEAD mandate. It now reads: 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> (working-tree diff — Phase 2 runs before staging and commit, so ..HEAD would return an empty set and falsely flag all legitimate uncommitted changes as anomalies)'. AC4 THEN clause specifies working-tree form and explicitly states '..HEAD form must NOT be used because Phase 2 runs before commit'. Edge Cases 'Dev commit timing' bullet correctly states 'changelog-analyst Phase 2 runs pre-commit' and refutes the erroneous post-commit notion — 'post-commit' appears only as a refuted claim.
finding_1_m5_head_form.ac4_test_status: The test_AC4 was also updated with semantic ..HEAD detection (per-line regex that exempts negation-context lines). It PASSES correctly despite ..HEAD appearing in the implementation as a refutation.
finding_1_m5_head_form.note: The AC JSON field 'pattern_absent_in_provenance_filter_step_1' is semantically imprecise (a naive grep would produce false failures), but the actual test implementation uses semantic detection that is correct. This is a minor spec_text_vs_test_implementation drift logged separately.

finding_2_files_created_coverage.what_was_fixed: M4 now has two sub-checks: (a) files_modified via git diff, (b) files_created via git ls-files --others --exclude-standard with label files_created_provenance_violation. AC3 THEN clause extended to cover both. AC6 added as standalone assertion for files_created path.
finding_2_files_created_coverage.what_was_not_fixed: M2 and agents/dev.md still say dev.files_created MUST be derived from git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>. Untracked new files do not appear in git diff output. This creates an internal inconsistency: QA checks files_created via ls-files (correct), but dev is told to populate it via git diff (incorrect for new files). Additionally, M5 and changelog-analyst.md step 3 apply the git diff filter to files_created without a separate ls-files check, meaning valid new files would be falsely classified as provenance_anomaly by changelog-analyst.

findings.finding: M2 mandates that BOTH dev.files_modified AND dev.files_created MUST be derived from 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>'. agents/dev.md carries the same instruction. However, git diff --name-only only shows tracked file changes — new untracked files (dev.files_created) do NOT appear in git diff output. The iter2 fix extended M4/AC3 to check files_created via git ls-files, but left M2 and agents/dev.md requiring git diff for files_created. If dev follows M2, files_created will be empty or wrong for cycles that genuinely create new files. QA's ls-files check would then have nothing to verify, and the provenance enforcement for files_created would be bypassed.
findings.proposed_fix: Update M2 to split the derivation rule: 'dev.files_modified MUST be derived from git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>; dev.files_created MUST be derived from git ls-files --others --exclude-standard (untracked files at end of dev execution).' Update agents/dev.md Git-diff derivation paragraph accordingly. This aligns with M4's CHECK method and with R3 which already specifies ls-files for files_created.

findings.finding: M5 specifies that paths in dev.files_modified / dev.files_created absent from 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>' AND not in baseline_dirty_snapshot are classified as provenance_anomaly. The changelog-analyst.md implementation (step 3) does the same: 'For every path in dev.files_modified and dev.files_created that is absent from git diff --name-only output AND absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot, classify as provenance_anomaly.' But new untracked files (dev.files_created) are by definition absent from git diff --name-only, so all legitimate files_created entries would be falsely classified as provenance_anomaly by changelog-analyst. Note: this is a PRE-EXISTING gap in M5 (not first introduced by iter2), but iter2's partial fix to M4/AC3 makes the inconsistency visible — M4 uses ls-files for files_created while M5 does not.
findings.proposed_fix: Update M5 to split the provenance filter for changelog-analyst: '(a) for paths in dev.files_modified, check absence from git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>; (b) for paths in dev.files_created, check absence from git ls-files --others --exclude-standard; in both cases also exclude paths in baseline_dirty_snapshot.' Update agents/changelog-analyst.md step 3 to use ls-files for the files_created subset.

findings.finding: AC4 check method is 'grep_and_absent' with field 'pattern_absent_in_provenance_filter_step_1: \.\.\.HEAD|\.\.HEAD'. A naive grep would falsely fail because ..HEAD appears in changelog-analyst.md:123 as a refutation ('..HEAD form is WRONG here'). The actual test (test_AC4) was updated with semantic per-line detection that correctly exempts refutation text. However, the AC JSON check field description does not match what the test actually implements — the field name implies a section-scoped absence check, not a semantic command-vs-explanation distinction.
findings.proposed_fix: Update AC4 check field to use 'pattern_absent_as_actual_diff_command' with pattern 'diff\s+--name-only\s+\S*\.\.\S*HEAD' (matches ..HEAD only when appearing as an actual git diff argument, not in explanatory text). Alternatively, document the semantic interpretation requirement in the 'expect' field.
findings.note: The test itself is correct and passes. This is a documentation/precision issue in the AC JSON check method.

passing_checks.m4_has_two_subchecks: CONFIRMED — M4 has (a) files_modified via git diff and (b) files_created via ls-files --others --exclude-standard
passing_checks.m4_files_created_provenance_violation_la: CONFIRMED — label 'files_created_provenance_violation' defined in M4 sub-check (b)
passing_checks.ac3_then_extends_to_files_created: CONFIRMED — AC3 THEN clause covers both (a) files_modified + dev_provenance_violation and (b) files_created + files_created_provenance_violation + ls-files

passing_checks.r3_source_phrase_updated: CONFIRMED — R3 now includes files_created_provenance_violation via ls-files in both ticket and context JSON

blockers.description: M2 (ticket) and agents/dev.md tell dev to derive files_created from git diff --name-only, which cannot enumerate new untracked files. M4 (QA check) uses ls-files for files_created. These rules are incompatible — a dev following M2 will produce incorrect/empty files_created for cycles that create new files, undermining the ls-files check in M4. BA must update M2 and agents/dev.md to specify git ls-files --others --exclude-standard for files_created derivation.
blockers.required_action: BA iter3: update M2 derivation rule to split files_modified (git diff) from files_created (git ls-files). Update agents/dev.md Git-diff derivation paragraph accordingly.

blockers.description: M5 and changelog-analyst.md step 3 apply git diff to files_created, which would falsely classify all legitimate new files as provenance_anomaly. BA must extend M5 (and update the implementation text in agents/changelog-analyst.md) to use ls-files for the files_created subset of the provenance filter.
blockers.required_action: BA iter3: update M5 provenance filter to split: files_modified via git diff, files_created via ls-files + baseline_dirty_snapshot exclusion. Update agents/changelog-analyst.md step 3 accordingly.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5 xhigh) provided 5 findings. (1) AC4 absent-pattern check is ambiguous for naive grep — PROPOSED_FIX: use command-scoped pattern. (2) M5/changelog-analyst mishandles files_created (git diff misses untracked files) — PROPOSED_FIX: split M5 into files_modified (git diff) vs files_created (ls-files). (3) M2/AC2/agents/dev.md says files_created derived from git diff — PROPOSED_FIX: update to specify ls-files for files_created. (4) AC6 redundancy is acceptable (OBSERVATION_ONLY). (5) Draft conclusion should FAIL given AC4 check ambiguity and files_created split gap.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: CF1 (AC4 executability): Confirmed the existing test was already updated with semantic detection that PASSES correctly. The AC JSON field is imprecise but the test is right — downgraded to minor F3. CF2 (M5 files_created gap): Confirmed as pre-existing gap now made inconsistent by iter2's partial fix — escalated to blocking F2. CF3 (M2 derivation gap): Confirmed as new inconsistency created by iter2's partial fix — escalated to blocking F1. CF4 (AC6 redundancy): Logged as acceptable per Codex OBSERVATION_ONLY verdict. CF5 (verdict): Adopted FAIL, though AC4 check ambiguity is minor; M2/M5 files_created inconsistency is the primary blocker.
codex_observation_only: AC6 is structurally redundant with AC3 (patterns subset) but acceptable as focused standalone assertion for the files_created path
